extraction.one_line_summary: PETERS Nans won from 13-rider early breakaway given 7+ minutes, while BARDET Roman attacked final climb to claw back 13 seconds lost from earlier crash.
tactical_patterns: MORKOV attacking intermediate sprint despite upcoming mountains positioned him ahead of potential breakaway splits—preemptive gap-bridging before splits materialize.
BARDET Roman's crash-recovery then final climb attack suggests AG2R prioritized regaining GC time over stage win despite teammate PETERS leading breakaway.
Breakaway gained 7:25 in first 2 miles with KOM leader COSNEFROY and GC-positioned DEIGNAN included—peloton conceded stage unusually early.
YATES Adam forced to chase BARDET personally on final climb rather than rely on teammates, revealing Mitchelton weakness in mountain domestique support.

rider_signals.BARDET Roman: positive: crashed with elbow injury on penultimate climb, then attacked final climb recovering all 13 seconds lost—exceptional recovery resilience under adversity.
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: 5th place finish extends strong breakaway positioning after yesterday's time loss, demonstrating intentional aggressive recovery tactics rather than defensive GC riding.
rider_signals.DEIGNAN Philip: neutral: included in 13-man breakaway as best-placed GC rider but finished 4th, suggesting breakaway was tactical positioning not genuine stage hunt.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: required personal chase of BARDET rather than team control on final climb, exposing thin mountain support despite holding yellow jersey.
rider_signals.COSNEFROY Benoit: positive: KOM leader positioned in stage-winning breakaway targeting 40 available points—proactive jersey defense rather than reactive marking.
